result:
queue = point-to-point, one consumer gets each message, work distribution
topic = pub/sub, every subscriber gets copy, fan-out events

pick queue when:
- task needs processing exactly once
- scaling workers horizontally (competing consumers)
- order/backpressure matters per worker
- job runners, email sends, payment processing

pick topic when:
- multiple independent systems react to same event
- decoupling producers from unknown consumer set
- audit/analytics/cache-invalidation siblings off same signal
- domain events ("order.placed" → billing + shipping + notifications)

hybrid common: topic fans out, each subscriber has own queue behind it (SNS→SQS, Kafka consumer groups). gives you fan-out across systems + load-balancing within each system.

rule of thumb: if adding second consumer means duplicating work, you want topic. if it means sharing work, you want queue.
